title: ODI Matrix — Steam and Air-Conditioning Supply (NAICS 221330)

sections.title: Step 1 — Error Statement Gathering
sections.content:
| Source | Count | Type |
|---|---|---|
| JTBD Step 06 (P2 core market needs) | 20 | product_related = false |
| JTBD Step 06 (P1 product lifecycle needs) | 9 | product_related = true |
| FIT Step 10 (product-constraint needs) | 7 | product_related = true (injected as job_step=product_constraint) |
| **Total** | **36** | — |

---

sections.title: Step 2 — Satisfaction Recalibration
sections.content:
Incumbent technology profiles used for recalibration:

| Incumbent | Share | Mechanism | Top Strengths | Top Weaknesses |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Mechanical Impeller / Turbine | 40–50% | Rotational (impeller) | Low unit cost, simple installation, proven MID certification | Magnetite fouling, bearing wear, calibration drift Class 2→3 in 3–5 yr, low-flow stall <1 l/min |
| Ultrasonic (Kamstrup MULTICAL) | 35–45% | Transit-time (acoustic) | No moving parts, stable long-term accuracy, MID/EN 1434 certified, 16-yr battery, combined flow+temp | Air entrainment >2% degrades accuracy, pipe-full required, 1.5–2.5x cost vs mechanical |
| Electromagnetic | 5–10% | Faraday induction | Unaffected by air/particles, high accuracy, wide turndown | 3–5x cost, requires conductivity >5 µS/cm, heavy/large, high power |

[SRC: JTBD_steam alternatives, FIT_steam vendor comparison]

### Recalibration Adjustments

| ID | Original Sat | Recalibrated Sat | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| N04 | 5 | 4 | Mechanical impellers (40–50% share) have inertia delay in stoppage detection; legacy installations often lack alarm integration. Market-weighted response time exceeds 5 s target. |
| N09 | 4 | 5 | Kamstrup handles glycol adequately; most district networks use treated water. Glycol compatibility increasingly addressed by ultrasonic incumbents. |
| N22 | 4 | 5 | Kamstrup provides combined flow+temp in single body. Growing ultrasonic share (35–45%) addresses this need. Market satisfaction improving. |
| N25 | 5 | 4 | Mechanical impellers exhibit 2–5% annual failure rate in aging networks from magnetite. Fleet-wide <0.1% target not achieved when mechanical dominates the installed base. |
| All others | — | Unchanged | Original Step 06 scores align with incumbent data from alternatives analysis. No contradictions found. |

---

sections.title: Step 3 — ODI Entries (Deterministic Computation)
sections.content:
**Formula:** `opportunity_score = importance + max(importance - satisfaction, 0)`
**Underserved:** `importance > 7 AND satisfaction < 5`
**Overserved:** `importance < 3 AND satisfaction > 7`

### Highly Underserved (opp > 15)

*None in this market.*

### Underserved (opp 12–15)

| ID | Statement | Step | Imp | Sat | Opp | Under | Over | Impact | Prod |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N01 | Minimize flow reading deviation >±2% per EN 1434 Class 2 across 0.5–60 l/min range | EXECUTE | 10 | 5 | 15.00 | false | false | reliability | false |
| N26 | Increase ability to deploy MID/EN 1434 billing-grade meters at every building connection for EU EED compliance | DEFINE | 10 | 5 | 15.00 | false | false | cost | false |
| N07 | Minimize undetected air entrainment >2% vol biasing billing-grade energy calculations | MONITOR | 9 | 3 | 15.00 | true | false | cost | false |
| N10 | Minimize low delta-T cooling circuit error amplification (3–8 K ΔT amplifies flow error 2–3x) | EXECUTE | 9 | 3 | 15.00 | true | false | cost | false |
| N11 | Minimize impeller rotor seizure from magnetite/calcium scale in aging district heating networks | Usage | 9 | 3 | 15.00 | true | false | reliability | true |
| N13 | Minimize calibration drift exceeding EN 1434 accuracy class from bearing wear/blade erosion | Usage | 9 | 3 | 15.00 | true | false | reliability | true |
| N03 | Minimize measurement degradation during seasonal temp swing 50–120 °C | EXECUTE | 9 | 4 | 14.00 | true | false | reliability | false |
| N04 | Minimize time to detect complete flow stoppage (valve failure, pipe freeze, pump trip) to <5 s | MONITOR | 9 | 4 | 14.00 | true | false | safety | false |
| N25 | Minimize field failure rate to <0.1% annually across 15-year fleet operating horizon | EXECUTE | 9 | 4 | 14.00 | true | false | reliability | false |
| N27 | Increase ability to demonstrate EU EED-compliant metering across 100% of connections with EN 1434 traceability | P3-ROLE | 9 | 4 | 14.00 | true | false | cost | false |
| N12 | Minimize fleet-wide 5–6 yr metrological re-verification and replacement cost (mechanical) | Maint. | 8 | 3 | 13.00 | true | false | cost | true |
| N14 | Increase ability to read accurately at <1 l/min where mechanical impellers stall | Usage | 8 | 3 | 13.00 | true | false | cost | true |
| N17 | Increase ability to distinguish zero-flow from ultrasonic signal loss (bubbles, empty pipe) | Usage | 8 | 3 | 13.00 | true | false | safety | true |
| PRN-01 | Minimize risk of polymer housing degradation at 90 °C winter peak-load conditions | product_constraint | 8 | 3 | 13.00 | true | false | reliability | true |
| N05 | Increase turndown ratio to cover winter peak and summer minimum in single meter | EXECUTE | 8 | 4 | 12.00 | true | false | reliability | false |
| N06 | Minimize likelihood of specifying a meter whose range misses building demand profile | DEFINE | 8 | 4 | 12.00 | true | false | cost | false |
| N08 | Minimize systematic error from non-ideal flow profiles in compact substation piping | EXECUTE | 8 | 4 | 12.00 | true | false | reliability | false |
| N18 | Minimize electromagnetic meter cost premium (3–5x) limiting deployment to large substations | Acq. | 7 | 2 | 12.00 | false | false | cost | true |

### Appropriately Served (opp 10–12)

| ID | Statement | Step | Imp | Sat | Opp | Under | Over | Impact | Prod |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N02 | Minimize flow reading variability under pulsating network-pressure conditions | EXECUTE | 8 | 5 | 11.00 | false | false | reliability | false |
| N22 | Increase ability to provide flow + temperature from single measurement point | EXECUTE | 8 | 5 | 11.00 | false | false | cost | false |
| PRN-04 | Minimize cumulative billing error from sub-threshold air (1–2% vol) that silently biases readings | product_constraint | 7 | 3 | 11.00 | false | false | reliability | true |
| N23 | Increase ability to detect incipient HX fouling via gradual flow-rate trend analysis | MONITOR | 7 | 3 | 11.00 | false | false | reliability | false |
| N09 | Minimize measurement failure rate in glycol antifreeze circuits (up to 40% concentration) | EXECUTE | 7 | 5 | 9.00 (recal) | false | false | reliability | false |  <!-- recal sat 4→5 -->
| N15 | Minimize signal loss from transient air pockets during venting/shutdown events (ultrasonic) | Usage | 7 | 4 | 10.00 | false | false | reliability | true |
| N24 | Minimize meter variants needed to cover DN15–DN50 range for heating + cooling | DEFINE | 7 | 4 | 10.00 | false | false | cost | false |
| N28 | Increase ability to provide occupant-accessible measured consumption data per billing period | P4-IMAGE | 7 | 4 | 10.00 | false | false | skill | false |
| N29 | Minimize billing disputes >5% of quarterly cost from meter inaccuracy or unmeasured periods | P5-EMOT | 7 | 4 | 10.00 | false | false | cost | false |
| PRN-03 | Minimize unmeasured energy below 4.2 l/min ultrasonic detection threshold (summer DHW standby) | product_constraint | 7 | 4 | 10.00 | false | false | reliability | true |

### Overserved / Low Priority (opp < 10)

| ID | Statement | Step | Imp | Sat | Opp | Under | Over | Impact | Prod |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| N19 | Minimize installation footprint in space-constrained building substations | LOCATE | 7 | 5 | 9.00 | false | false | cost | false |
| N20 | Minimize total integration time per substation (unpack to first valid reading) | PREPARE | 7 | 5 | 9.00 | false | false | time | false |
| PRN-02 | Minimize deployment exclusion at substations exceeding 10-bar operating pressure | product_constraint | 6 | 3 | 9.00 | false | false | reliability | true |
| PRN-06 | Minimize single-chord path bias from insufficient straight-run (<5x DN) | product_constraint | 7 | 5 | 9.00 | false | false | reliability | true |
| N16 | Minimize ultrasonic commissioning time (acoustic coupling, fluid config, calculator handshake) | Prep. | 6 | 5 | 7.00 | false | false | time | true |
| PRN-07 | Minimize speed-of-sound error from rapid temp transients (>5 °C/min) outpacing NTC compensation | product_constraint | 6 | 5 | 7.00 | false | false | reliability | true |
| N21 | Minimize commissioning sign-off with incorrect sensor orientation (forward/reverse) | CONFIRM | 6 | 6 | 6.00 | false | false | skill | false |
| PRN-05 | Minimize measurement blackout during partial pipe fill (network maintenance drain) | product_constraint | 5 | 4 | 6.00 | false | false | reliability | true |

---

sections.title: Step 4 — Market ODI Summary
sections.content:
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Market** | Steam and Air-Conditioning Supply |
| **NAICS** | 221330 |
| **Total Needs Scored** | 36 |
| **Underserved Count** | 15 |
| **Overserved Count** | 0 |
| **Avg Opportunity Score** | 11.42 |

### Top 5 Opportunities

| Rank | ID | Opp | Statement |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | N01 | 15.00 | Minimize flow reading deviation >±2% per EN 1434 Class 2 across 0.5–60 l/min operating range |
| 2 | N26 | 15.00 | Increase ability to deploy MID/EN 1434 billing-grade meters at every building connection |
| 3 | N07 | 15.00 | Minimize undetected air entrainment >2% vol biasing billing-grade energy calculations |
| 4 | N10 | 15.00 | Minimize low delta-T cooling circuit error amplification (flow error 2–3x in 3–8 K ΔT) |
| 5 | N11 | 15.00 | Minimize impeller rotor seizure from magnetite/calcium scale in aging networks |

*Tie-breaking: importance descending (N01=10, N26=10, N07=9, N10=9, N11=9), then ID ascending. N13 (15.00, imp=9) is 6th.*

### Opportunity Zone Distribution

| Zone | Score Range | Count | % |
|---|---|---|---|
| Highly Underserved | > 15 | 0 | 0% |
| Underserved | 12–15 | 18 | 50% |
| Appropriately Served | 10–12 | 10 | 28% |
| Overserved | < 10 | 8 | 22% |

---

sections.title: Step 5 — Cross-Check: ODI vs Feature-Market Fit
sections.content:
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| ODI avg opportunity score | 11.42 |
| Feature-Fit avg (Step 09) | 5.20 |
| Threshold: ODI > 12 AND Fit < 4 | NOT triggered |
| Threshold: ODI < 8 AND Fit > 7 | NOT triggered |
| **Result** | **No inconsistencies** |

The ODI average (11.42) is just below the high-opportunity threshold (12), and feature-fit (5.20) is moderate. This aligns: the market has significant unmet needs (15 underserved), and the ultrasonic technology addresses them moderately well — strong on reliability/maintenance (No Moving Parts: 6.44, Digital Anomaly: 6.44) but limited on low-priority dimensions (Zero Pressure Drop: 2.89).

---

sections.title: QA Checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Pass Condition | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | Formula correct | Spot-check 5 entries: opp = imp + max(imp - sat, 0) | **PASS** — N01: 10+max(5,0)=15 ✓; N07: 9+max(6,0)=15 ✓; N21: 6+max(0,0)=6 ✓; PRN-05: 5+max(1,0)=6 ✓; N18: 7+max(5,0)=12 ✓ |
| Q2 | Underserved flags correct | imp > 7 AND sat < 5 ↔ is_underserved | **PASS** — 15 entries flagged; verified N01 (imp=10, sat=5 → false, correct); N07 (imp=9, sat=3 → true, correct); N09 (imp=7, sat=5 → false, correct) |
| Q3 | Overserved flags correct | imp < 3 AND sat > 7 ↔ is_overserved | **PASS** — 0 entries; no need has imp < 3 |
| Q4 | Need count preserved | Total ≥ JTBD count (29) + Step 10 may add more | **PASS** — 29 + 7 = 36 |
| Q5 | Top-5 ordering correct | Sorted by -opp, -imp, +id | **PASS** — all at 15.00; tie-broken by importance |
| Q6 | Cross-check ran | ODI vs Feature-Fit thresholds checked | **PASS** — no inconsistencies flagged |

---

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
- JTBD_steam_and_air_conditioning_supply.md — Error statements N01-N29, alternatives, incumbent profiles
- FIT_steam_and_air_conditioning_supply.md — Product-related needs PRN-01 to PRN-07, technology feature-market fit scores, vendor comparison (Kamstrup MULTICAL)
- 01_product_decomposition.md — Product constraints, specifications, features
- 02_functional_promise.md — Commodity FP, FP extension
- 05_market_discovery.md — NAICS 221330 candidate detail, architecture distance scoring

rows.Top Strengths: Low unit cost, simple installation, proven MID certification
rows.Top Weaknesses: Magnetite fouling, bearing wear, calibration drift Class 2→3 in 3–5 yr, low-flow stall <1 l/min

rows.Top Strengths: No moving parts, stable long-term accuracy, MID/EN 1434 certified, 16-yr battery, combined flow+temp
rows.Top Weaknesses: Air entrainment >2% degrades accuracy, pipe-full required, 1.5–2.5x cost vs mechanical

rows.Top Strengths: Unaffected by air/particles, high accuracy, wide turndown
rows.Top Weaknesses: 3–5x cost, requires conductivity >5 µS/cm, heavy/large, high power

rows.Rationale: Mechanical impellers (40–50% share) have inertia delay in stoppage detection; legacy installations often lack alarm integration. Market-weighted response time exceeds 5 s target.
Kamstrup handles glycol adequately; most district networks use treated water. Glycol compatibility increasingly addressed by ultrasonic incumbents.
Kamstrup provides combined flow+temp in single body. Growing ultrasonic share (35–45%) addresses this need. Market satisfaction improving.
Mechanical impellers exhibit 2–5% annual failure rate in aging networks from magnetite. Fleet-wide <0.1% target not achieved when mechanical dominates the installed base.
Original Step 06 scores align with incumbent data from alternatives analysis. No contradictions found.

rows.Statement: Minimize flow reading deviation >±2% per EN 1434 Class 2 across 0.5–60 l/min range
Increase ability to deploy MID/EN 1434 billing-grade meters at every building connection for EU EED compliance
Minimize undetected air entrainment >2% vol biasing billing-grade energy calculations
Minimize low delta-T cooling circuit error amplification (3–8 K ΔT amplifies flow error 2–3x)
Minimize impeller rotor seizure from magnetite/calcium scale in aging district heating networks
Minimize calibration drift exceeding EN 1434 accuracy class from bearing wear/blade erosion
Minimize measurement degradation during seasonal temp swing 50–120 °C
Minimize time to detect complete flow stoppage (valve failure, pipe freeze, pump trip) to <5 s
Minimize field failure rate to <0.1% annually across 15-year fleet operating horizon
Increase ability to demonstrate EU EED-compliant metering across 100% of connections with EN 1434 traceability
Minimize fleet-wide 5–6 yr metrological re-verification and replacement cost (mechanical)
Increase ability to read accurately at <1 l/min where mechanical impellers stall
Increase ability to distinguish zero-flow from ultrasonic signal loss (bubbles, empty pipe)
Minimize risk of polymer housing degradation at 90 °C winter peak-load conditions
Increase turndown ratio to cover winter peak and summer minimum in single meter
Minimize likelihood of specifying a meter whose range misses building demand profile
Minimize systematic error from non-ideal flow profiles in compact substation piping
Minimize electromagnetic meter cost premium (3–5x) limiting deployment to large substations
Minimize flow reading variability under pulsating network-pressure conditions
Increase ability to provide flow + temperature from single measurement point
Minimize cumulative billing error from sub-threshold air (1–2% vol) that silently biases readings
Increase ability to detect incipient HX fouling via gradual flow-rate trend analysis
Minimize measurement failure rate in glycol antifreeze circuits (up to 40% concentration)
Minimize installation footprint in space-constrained building substations
Minimize total integration time per substation (unpack to first valid reading)
Minimize deployment exclusion at substations exceeding 10-bar operating pressure
Minimize single-chord path bias from insufficient straight-run (<5x DN)
Minimize ultrasonic commissioning time (acoustic coupling, fluid config, calculator handshake)
Minimize speed-of-sound error from rapid temp transients (>5 °C/min) outpacing NTC compensation
Minimize commissioning sign-off with incorrect sensor orientation (forward/reverse)
Minimize measurement blackout during partial pipe fill (network maintenance drain)
Minimize flow reading deviation >±2% per EN 1434 Class 2 across 0.5–60 l/min operating range
Increase ability to deploy MID/EN 1434 billing-grade meters at every building connection
Minimize undetected air entrainment >2% vol biasing billing-grade energy calculations
Minimize low delta-T cooling circuit error amplification (flow error 2–3x in 3–8 K ΔT)
Minimize impeller rotor seizure from magnetite/calcium scale in aging networks

rows.Pass Condition: Spot-check 5 entries: opp = imp + max(imp - sat, 0)

rows.Result: **PASS** — 15 entries flagged; verified N01 (imp=10, sat=5 → false, correct); N07 (imp=9, sat=3 → true, correct); N09 (imp=7, sat=5 → false, correct)

rows.Pass Condition: Total ≥ JTBD count (29) + Step 10 may add more

incumbents_used.name: Mechanical Impeller / Turbine Meter
weaknesses: calibration drift Class 2 to 3 in 3-5 yr

adjustments.rationale: Mechanical impellers have inertia delay in stoppage detection; legacy installations lack alarm integration
Kamstrup handles glycol adequately; glycol compatibility increasingly addressed by ultrasonic incumbents
Kamstrup provides combined flow+temp in single body; growing ultrasonic share addresses this need
Mechanical impellers exhibit 2-5% annual failure rate; fleet-wide <0.1% target not achieved

entries.error_statement: Minimize the likelihood of volumetric flow reading deviating more than ±2% from true value across the 0.5-60 l/min operating range per EN 1434 Class 2 requirements
Minimize variability of flow readings between successive measurement cycles under pulsating network-pressure conditions (repeatability >±0.5%)
Minimize measurement degradation when supply temperature shifts from 50 °C (summer DHW) to 120 °C (winter peak) across seasonal operating modes
Minimize time to detect a complete flow stoppage event (valve failure, pipe freeze, pump trip) to less than 5 seconds for safety interlock and billing integrity
Increase the turndown ratio (max/min measurable flow) to cover both winter peak-load and summer minimum-load operating points in a single meter without range switching
Minimize the likelihood of specifying a meter whose flow range does not cover the building's actual demand profile, causing unmeasured energy delivery during low-load hours
Minimize the number of undetected air entrainment events exceeding 2% volume that silently bias billing-grade energy calculations
Minimize systematic measurement error caused by non-ideal flow profiles from upstream elbows, valves, and tees in compact substation piping layouts
Minimize measurement failure rate when medium contains glycol antifreeze (up to 40% concentration) in district cooling and frost-protected circuits

Increase the ability to accurately calculate thermal energy (Q = V x rho x Cp x delta-T) in district cooling circuits where delta-T is only 3-8 K, amplifying flow measurement error by 2-3x vs heating circuits
Minimize the likelihood of impeller rotor seizure from magnetite and calcium scale accumulation in aging district heating networks requiring unplanned meter replacement
Minimize the fleet-wide maintenance cost of mandatory 5-6 year metrological re-verification and replacement cycles across hundreds of building substations
Minimize calibration drift exceeding one EN 1434 accuracy class over the certification period caused by bearing wear and blade erosion in hard-water networks

Increase the ability to maintain accurate readings at low flow rates (<1 l/min) where mechanical impeller rotors stall due to friction, causing unmeasured energy leakage during summer low-load periods
Minimize the likelihood of complete signal loss when transient air pockets pass through the sensor during network venting, seasonal shutdown, or pressure fluctuation events
Minimize the commissioning time required to verify acoustic coupling, configure fluid parameters, and validate communication with the energy calculator per substation
Increase the ability to distinguish genuine zero-flow conditions from ultrasonic signal loss caused by transient medium anomalies (bubbles, empty pipe, acoustic decoupling)
Minimize the per-substation cost premium (3-5x vs ultrasonic) that limits electromagnetic meter deployment to only the largest commercial substations, leaving smaller connections underserved
Minimize the installation footprint (physical length and required straight-pipe run) to enable placement in space-constrained building substations where piping is tightly packed
Minimize the total integration time from unpacking to first valid energy reading, including mechanical fitting, electrical connection, calculator handshake, and MID seal application
Minimize the likelihood of commissioning sign-off with incorrect sensor orientation (forward/reverse) that inverts flow direction in the energy calculation
Increase the ability to simultaneously provide flow rate AND medium temperature from a single measurement point, eliminating separate temperature probe installation and reducing substation complexity
Increase the ability to detect incipient heat exchanger fouling via gradual flow-rate reduction trend analysis before thermal performance degrades below billing-relevant thresholds
Minimize the number of meter variants a district energy operator must qualify, stock, and manage to cover its building portfolio (DN15-DN50 range, heating + cooling)
Minimize the field failure rate of the flow metering subsystem to below 0.1% annually across the fleet of building substations over a 15-year operating horizon
Increase the ability to deploy MID/EN 1434-certified billing-grade meters at every building connection to achieve full EU EED compliance across the entire district network
Increase the ability to demonstrate EU EED-compliant individual consumption metering across 100% of building connections during regulatory audit, with verifiable metrological traceability per EN 1434

Increase the ability to provide building-owner-accessible consumption data (display, remote reading, or portal) that demonstrates measured — not estimated — thermal energy delivery for each billing period
Minimize the likelihood of billing disputes exceeding 5% of quarterly energy cost caused by meter inaccuracy or unmeasured consumption periods that erode tenant trust in the district energy provider

Minimize the risk of polymer housing and seal degradation when district heating supply temperatures approach the 90°C material limit during winter peak-load conditions, reducing sensor service life below the 15-year fleet expectation
Minimize the deployment exclusion at building substations where network operating pressure exceeds the 10-bar polymer housing structural rating during peak demand or water-hammer events
Minimize the unmeasured energy delivery below the ultrasonic transit-time signal-to-noise detection threshold (4.2 l/min for DN20) during summer domestic hot water standby operation

Minimize the cumulative billing error from degraded ultrasonic transit-time accuracy during sub-threshold air entrainment events (1-2% by volume) that do not trigger signal-loss alarms but silently bias flow readings
Minimize the duration of measurement blackout when ultrasonic signal is lost due to partial pipe fill during network maintenance operations that temporarily drain the pipe section

Minimize the systematic measurement bias from single-chord acoustic path sampling in asymmetric flow profiles caused by insufficient upstream straight-pipe run (<5x DN) in compact building substations
Minimize residual flow measurement error from speed-of-sound variation during rapid supply-temperature transients (>5°C/min) that outpace the NTC thermistor compensation response time

top_opportunities: Minimize the likelihood of volumetric flow reading deviating more than ±2% from true value across the 0.5-60 l/min operating range per EN 1434 Class 2 requirements
Increase the ability to deploy MID/EN 1434-certified billing-grade meters at every building connection to achieve full EU EED compliance across the entire district network
Minimize the number of undetected air entrainment events exceeding 2% volume that silently bias billing-grade energy calculations
Increase the ability to accurately calculate thermal energy in district cooling circuits where delta-T is only 3-8 K, amplifying flow measurement error by 2-3x vs heating circuits
Minimize the likelihood of impeller rotor seizure from magnetite and calcium scale accumulation in aging district heating networks requiring unplanned meter replacement
